title: Abnormal uterine bleeding
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Specifies bleeding nature, persistent intermenstrual bleeding, pelvic pain or pressure, quality-of-life impact, comorbidities, prior treatment, and examination context.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to clinician-entered bleeding pattern, impact and examination context.

source.title: Heavy menstrual bleeding: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Women of reproductive age with heavy menstrual bleeding, including selected persistent intermenstrual or irregular bleeding contexts.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of heavy menstrual bleeding.
source.exclusions: Exact for heavy menstrual bleeding and selected persistent irregular or intermenstrual bleeding contexts; partial for undifferentiated abnormal uterine bleeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies bleeding nature, persistent intermenstrual bleeding, pelvic pain or pressure, quality-of-life impact, comorbidities, prior treatment, and examination context.

assessment.text: Requires full blood count, limits coagulation testing to relevant history, and states that ferritin, hormone, and thyroid testing are not routine without specific indications.
assessment.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-selected or reviewed tests.

source.title: Heavy menstrual bleeding: assessment and management — Recommendations
source.population: Women of reproductive age with heavy menstrual bleeding, including selected persistent intermenstrual or irregular bleeding contexts.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of heavy menstrual bleeding.
source.exclusions: Exact for heavy menstrual bleeding and selected persistent irregular or intermenstrual bleeding contexts; partial for undifferentiated abnormal uterine bleeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires full blood count, limits coagulation testing to relevant history, and states that ferritin, hormone, and thyroid testing are not routine without specific indications.

assessment.text: Links history and examination to ultrasound or hysteroscopy selection and identifies persistent intermenstrual or irregular bleeding among endometrial-pathology risk contexts.
assessment.rationale: Relevant only after history and examination establish the recommendation context.
